subject: Join us - 2015 Robin Hood Investors Conference
body:
We are pleased, again this year, to extend a special early invitation to you for our Third Annual 
http://investors.robinhood.org/ 
Robin Hood Investors Conference , an event we are personally proud to be involved with. 

You may recall the past two years, our two-day program has produced keen investor insights, spirited presentations and debates, and truly inspiring results--not just stock picks, which have collectively outpaced the S&P by nearly 2:1; but also, the money raised: a whopping $11.8 million. As with all things Robin Hood, 100% of ticket sales go towards helping New Yorkers living in poverty to build better lives for themselves and their families. 

The Robin Hood Investors Conference is not like any financial summit we know. This event involves some of the most influential hedge-fund managers, policy experts, financial leaders, tech innovators and real estate investors to debate, discuss and divulge their views on today's markets. We're still putting together a final list of 
http://investors.robinhood.org/speakers 
presenters , but all of them are icons. Just seeing Paul Tudor Jones and Stan Druckenmiller, the godfathers of our industry, in a rare, intimate conversation is worth the ticket price alone! 

The dates of the Investors Conference are November 16-17, 2015; the event will be hosted by Hyatt at the Grand Hyatt New York. This year's program is shaping up to be even more insightful, provocative and actionable than in years' past, and I certainly hope you can join us. One foregone conclusion: demand for tickets is high and the event will sell out. If you're planning to attend, I urge you to 
http://rhicregistration.robinhood.org/events/2015-robin-hood-investors-conference/registration-8d52ce8e02754419aa286aa71ae46d9b.aspx 
purchase your tickets now , before general ticket sales open up in about a week.
We look forward to seeing you there.
John A. Griffin, Philippe P. Laffont, Daniel S. Loeb and Barry S. Sternlicht

The Robin Hood Investors Conference Committee 

ROBIN HOOD | 826 Broadway, 9th Floor, New York, NY 10003, USA | 
http://www.robinhood.org/ 
robinhood.org 

http://cl.s6.exct.net/profile_center.aspx?s=fe5e1076766d027d7511&mid=fe9312727666077e7c&j=fecc16737667067b&l=ff2c1c717360&jb=ffcf14&ju=

We are pleased, again this year, to extend a special early invitation to you for our Third Annual Robin Hood Investors Conference, an event we are personally proud to be involved with. 

You may recall the past two years, our two-day program has produced keen investor insights, spirited presentations and debates, and truly inspiring results--not just stock picks, which have collectively outpaced the S&P by nearly 2:1; but also, the money raised: a whopping $11.8 million. As with all things Robin Hood, 100% of ticket sales go towards helping New Yorkers living in poverty to build better lives for themselves and their families. 

The Robin Hood Investors Conference is not like any financial summit we know. This event involves some of the most influential hedge-fund managers, policy experts, financial leaders, tech innovators and real estate investors to debate, discuss and divulge their views on today's markets. We're still putting together a final list of presenters, but all of them are icons. Just seeing Paul Tudor Jones and Stan Druckenmiller, the godfathers of our industry, in a rare, intimate conversation is worth the ticket price alone! 

The dates of the Investors Conference are November 16-17, 2015; the event will be hosted by Hyatt at the Grand Hyatt New York. This year's program is shaping up to be even more insightful, provocative and actionable than in years' past, and I certainly hope you can join us. One foregone conclusion: demand for tickets is high and the event will sell out. If you're planning to attend, I urge you to purchase your tickets now, before general ticket sales open up in about a week.

We look forward to seeing you there.

John A. Griffin, Philippe P. Laffont, Daniel S. Loeb and Barry S. Sternlicht
